overview.description: AI for games research group at Charles University Faculty of Mathematics and Physics
overview.description_long: Game Development @ MFF-UK is a research and education group at Charles University (Faculty of Mathematics and Physics, Prague) focused on AI for games, AI-aided game development, applied AI in games, and AI interaction in games. The group runs game programming courses, organises game jams, participates in Erasmus+ projects on game dev higher education, and conducts research in generative AI, reinforcement learning, human-AI interaction, and agentic AI applied to the gaming domain.
overview.tagline: Looking into the Future of Gaming

history.origin_story: Established at Charles University Faculty of Mathematics and Physics in Prague; courses documented from 2013-2014 onwards. Focuses on bridging academic research and game development industry.

key_milestones.event: First game development courses at MFF-UK
Started hosting Ludum Dare game jams at MFF
GAMEHIGHED Erasmus+ project launched (2019-2022), partnering with 4 European universities

grants.name: GAMEHIGHED
grants.description: Higher-ed programmes for careers in game design and development; 5-partner consortium

grants.name: Gamebadges
grants.description: Active research project on gamification/badges in game development education

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Academic research group at Charles University; not a commercial entity. Research mission is to explore and advance AI in games, not a product that could be displaced.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Research group embedded within a university; not subject to market risk in the commercial sense. Funding comes from Erasmus+ grants and university budget.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Funded through Charles University and EU Erasmus+ grants. Active grant projects (Gamebadges 2024-2027) indicate continued institutional support.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Academic research group within Charles University's Faculty of Mathematics and Physics. Risk framework designed for commercial entities does not apply.

metadata.notes: CNAIP categorises this as 'Research groups', not a company — index category 'company' appears to be a data entry error. This is a game development research and education group led by Vojtěch Černý at Charles University MFF. Website (gamedev.cuni.cz) is active with courses through 2025-2026 but blog last updated 2018.
